idealClaims.text: A good extraction benchmark should separate claim-worthy statements from boilerplate, fragments, and sponsor copy.

children.text: Boilerplate intros, outros, and call-to-action lines should be rejected.
Fragments without a complete assertion should also be rejected.
Sponsor copy should be treated as non-claim content.

idealClaims.text: Synthetic nutrition and exercise claims can still be used to validate extraction hierarchy and evidence handling.

children.text: A claim about daily protein distribution can be classified separately from a claim about training recovery.
Short factual statements should remain atomic rather than being merged into one long summary sentence.

idealClaims.text: A representative panel benchmark should stress multi-speaker attribution and turn-taking.

children.text: Speaker changes should be preserved when the transcript alternates quickly between panelists.
Show-note citations should be extracted when they are stated as explicit references.

idealClaims.text: A panel benchmark should include both concise claims and longer reasoning chains to test hierarchy handling.

children.text: Concise claims should remain standalone when they can be judged independently.
Longer reasoning chains should still preserve the original claim order.

rejectedClaims.text: We'll pause here and continue later.
